rationale: The cited paper is highly aligned with the citing paper’s core problem: both analyze geographically varying offline-to-online substitution in e-commerce demand (cited note: preference isolation mechanism; citing note: local conditions for substitution vs. complementarity). Given this overlap, the 2011 paper is plausibly a foundational/comparison source for the citing paper’s literature positioning and mechanism discussion, not an obviously incidental mention. Although the raw match is only DOI-level, there is no clear evidence it is merely perfunctory.
